entry.title: Kickstart SAP TechEd 2024 with Autonomous Finance and Maximize Your Business Efficiency
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is here! As we kick off for this pivotal event in the SAP calendar, anticipation is building around the revolutionary talks and groundbreaking ideas set to be unveiled. For this year’s fully virtual TechEd, I’m excited to share the new SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) use case, Autonomous Finance.

 

What is Autonomous Finance?

So, what exactly is autonomous finance, and why is it becoming a game-changer in the world of oCFO? Gartner states that autonomous finance is a “finance function where processes and activities are partly governed and majority operated by self-learning software agents that optimize front-, middle- and back-office operations." Powered by technologies like AI, machine learning, and automation, it will affect all parts of the finance function under the office of the CFO. 

So why should businesses be excited about autonomous finance? Let's examine some of its compelling advantages.

  1. Autonomy in finance drastically reduces human intervention, which translates into significant cost reductions for organizations.
  2. It also speeds up operations and boosts productivity.
  3. AI-driven analytics can provide deep, actionable insights from financial data, empowering businesses to make smarter, strategic decisions.

Take a look at this infographic on the trusted path to autonomous finance. 

 

Customer Success Stories

Several SAP customers are already tapping into the potential of Autonomous Finance to propel their businesses forward:

  • Connect departments: Endress+Hauser significantly boosted overall efficiency and simplified operations by utilizing SAP Integration Suite’s tools to standardize their data sources. Thanks to the suite’s APIs and capabilities, Endress+Hauser's customers can now effortlessly place equipment orders directly from their ERP systems. This, in turn, automatically generates a production order within Endress Hauser’s ERP system and forwards it to the production facility. 
    • Aligned priority use case: Deliver Connected Experiences - Accelerate integration and increase productivity
  • Analyze: ARAG adopted SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud to create a unified and efficient analytics solution, enabling easy data accessibility. The combination of these technologies enabled ARAG to make all their data more accessible across different countries and teams, which paved the way for faster gaining of meaningful insights.
    • Aligned priority use case 1: Integrate hyperscaler data with SAP Datasphere - Deliver insights using live data from SAP and hyperscalers
    • Aligned priority use case 2: Accelerate Financial Planning and Analysis - Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis
  • Automate processes: Orbico's IT team implemented SAP Build Process Automation to automate specific processes and tasks, thereby reducing project times and costs. The team launched over 40 process automation projects within a year, including a bot that automated invoice processing from PDFs into SAP S/4HANA, recognizing invoices from 20 vendors.
    • Aligned priority use case: Increase efficiency and accuracy while reducing costs - Automate processes to empower finance professionals
  • Embed intelligence: To Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One Team, efficiency is key to success. Formula One has cost caps that limit each team’s spending on cars per season with severe penalties for overshoots, making financial management one of their biggest challenges. Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Team will leverage SAP S/4HANA Finance and SAP Business AI to optimize resource management and cost forecasting.
    • Aligned priority use case: Enhance customer support using the power of generative AI - Improve operations and decision-making with the power of generative AI

 

How to get started

SAP Discovery Center is an incredible asset to any business. If you’re in search of ways to start deriving value with SAP Business Technology Platform for your business, this is the place to start. It empowers users to easily uncover, find, and embark on SAP Business Technology Platform use cases.

Why SAP Discovery Center?

  1. SAP Discovery Center offers a catalogue of hundreds of detailed and comprehensive missions. These missions cover numerous aspects of business to inspire you on your SAP BTP journey. For example, when you’re ready to embark on the Autonomous Finance journey, these Discovery Center Missions will help you to take each step and make it best suited to your business needs.
  2. It provides guidance on how to use the wide range of services offered by SAP. This step-by-step guidance means you can use SAP’s powerful suite of tools to the best of your abilities and utilize the Free Tier and/or Free Trial.
  3. It encourages learning and exploration. With SAP Discovery Center, you not only understand how a host of innovative technologies could potentially benefit your business but also how to implement them efficiently for the best results with the provided reference architecture.
  4. The collaborative feature of SAP Discovery Center is very important. Knowledge and experiences are shared, thus enhancing productivity and enabling synergy. You can see which existing SAP customers have already implemented the use case of your interest and connect with SAP experts if you have any questions on the Mission.

Using SAP Discovery Center means you’re leveraging not just a tool but a collaborative platform that guides innovation, encourages learning, and boosts efficiency. Make sure you're tuned in for the XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center session by Nis Boy Naeve and Ingo Deck from SAP Discovery Center team to learn more. 

 

What else can I do with SAP Business Technology Platform?

If you are interested in understanding further what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for your business, check out our comprehensive use case one-pager. The document is designed to provide a clear outline of how SAP BTP can be utilized to enhance your business operations, per your department. Grasping the use cases will allow your business to leverage SAP BTP effectively to drive business performance and inform your strategy, discover new potential, and maximize your technology investment with our SAP BTP use case one-pager. Don’t forget to also check out SAP Business Technology Platform use cases on the website.

You can read further on the SAP Business Technology Platform Priority Use Case One-Pager on this blog article.

 

Get ready for SAP TechEd 2024

I hope this got you excited about SAP TechEd 2024. Don't miss this opportunity to be part of a transformative event, and make sure to register for the keynote session with Muhammad Alam. Register now to secure your spot, and add the following recommended sessions to your agenda.

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
  • DA180 | Explore SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud end to end
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • AI194 | SAP Business AI and Joule: What’s in it for innovators? 
  • XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center

 

We look forward to connecting with you!

entry.title: 🚀Fire of Innovations: Highlights from SAP TechEd 2024 📢
entry.content:
The SAP TechEd 2024 event, themed “Fire of Innovations,” has once again set the stage for groundbreaking advancements in enterprise technology. Held virtually on October 8-9, this year’s conference showcased a plethora of new tools, updates, and strategies designed to empower businesses and developers alike. Here’s a deep dive into some of the most exciting announcements and innovations from the event.

1. Joule: The AI Copilot Revolution

One of the standout highlights was the unveiling of new capabilities for Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot. Joule has been supercharged with collaborative AI agents that can perform complex, cross-disciplinary tasks. These agents are designed to break down silos within organizations, enhancing productivity and allowing human workers to focus on more creative and strategic activities.

For instance, in dispute management, autonomous AI agents can now analyze and resolve issues such as incorrect invoices or duplicate payments. In financial accounting, these agents streamline processes like bill payments and ledger updates, quickly addressing any inconsistencies.

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Enhancements

SAP continues to expand its Business Technology Platform (BTP), which is central to its cloud strategy. This year, SAP announced the expansion of BTP coverage through eight new SAP-managed data centers. Additionally, the SAP Discovery Center now offers a Quick-Account-Setup feature, enabling businesses to deploy specific use cases with just a click.

The integration of ABAP Cloud into SAP Build was another significant update. This integration allows developers to create and maintain ABAP Cloud projects directly from the SAP Build lobby, ensuring a seamless and efficient development process.

3. Innovations in SAP Build

SAP Build has been strengthened to support a “Clean Core” strategy, which is crucial for maintaining system consistency and upgradeability. The SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, introduced for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, helps both key users and developers create on-stack and side-by-side extensions in an embedded experience.

4. Harnessing the Power of Data with SAP Knowledge Graph

SAP introduced the SAP Knowledge Graph, a next-generation solution that connects SAP data with rich business context. This tool is poised to help developers unlock the full value of SAP data, driving more informed decision-making and innovation.

5. Global Reach and Accessibility

This year’s SAP TechEd was a truly global event, with a free two-day virtual format that included keynotes, technical sessions, and developer enablement activities. The event was designed to be accessible to a worldwide audience, ensuring that everyone could benefit from the latest SAP innovations.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 has once again demonstrated SAP’s commitment to driving innovation and empowering businesses with cutting-edge technology. From AI advancements with Joule to enhancements in SAP BTP and SAP Build, the “Fire of Innovations” theme truly encapsulates the transformative potential of these new tools and strategies. As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of the digital age, SAP’s innovations provide a robust foundation for growth and success.

Stay tuned for more updates and insights from SAP TechEd as we continue to explore the future of enterprise technology.

Feel free to share your thoughts or ask any questions about the event! What innovation are you most excited about?

entry.title: SAP Developer News: TechEd Day 1
entry.content:
Description

SAP TechEd General

  • News Guide: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide.html
  • TechEd Watch Live: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

Build Code Enhancements for Developers

  • Application Development and Automation News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/application-development-and-automation.html
  • SAP Build Extensibility Wizard: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-extensibility-wizard-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13867427
  • SAP Build Code Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241008&search.offeringsproducts=1698907352855001TA5c

AI Innovations – Part 1

  • Artificial Intelligence News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/ai.html
  • AI Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.track=1692371026432002VTW0

AI Innovations – Part 2

  • Artificial Intelligence News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/ai.html
  • AI Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.track=1692371026432002VTW0

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 TechEd Intro
1:09 Build Code Enhancements for Developers
3:30 AI Innovations – Part 1
4:33 AI Innovations – Part 2

Transcript

[Intro] Well, a developer in his houseboat, innovating cutting-edge tech from the high seas. He could have kept his anchors away, but he didn't.

[DJ] So while the crew were at lunch we've snuck into the studios to record this item on the couch. So we're at TechEd as we can see here I've got some a card here and one of the items announced at TechEd as you may know is that there are new capabilities that have been added and will be added to SAP Build the complete solution for building end-to-end extensions on SAP S/4 HANA Cloud and on other line of business applications. There are three here written down that I want to highlight.

There's a new extension wizard and it allows you to jump directly from SAP S/4 HANA Cloud public edition straight to SAP Build. Why is that important? Because there's a lot of context within the context of the application and that context objects events and so on business data is super important not to lose that so this new extension wizard which is available for building side-by-side and on stack extensions allows that jump including the context there are new generative AI capabilities with dual coming in this quarter for example alongside the already existing code generation facilities, there are or there will be code explanation facilities and also document search. I think they're great complements to what already exists.

On the low code arena, there are also dual enhancements to help with the creation of UIs and workflow automation.

And last but not least, there's now direct access or there will be, by the end of this year, direct access from SAP Build to ABAP development environments in the cloud and on BTP. So basically the whole idea is that this is one step closer to a more unified development environment. You can jump from the lobby to SAP Build Code, SAP Build Apps and now to specific ABAP development environments and this helps enormously with success for teams especially in fusion development contexts. Anyway check out the announcements from SAP TechEd for more details on these new SAP build capabilities.

[Nora] There's a lot happening with AI at TechEd at the moment and you've heard a lot of news and I'm just going to try to put as much as possible into the next couple of minutes. So there has been a lot of rumor on the SAP Foundation model for a while now and it's getting real and if you want to know more about the SAP Foundation model you should definitely check out the session AI 102 on Wednesday. SAP Foundation Model and Knowledge Graph, where you can learn about the SAP Foundation Model and how it can actually interpret structured data and fill in missing data points. Then we also have a lot of new features in the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core. Mainly we have new models coming up, so we will have the IBM Granite model series. We will also have the Dali 3 model and the Amazon Titan image generator model. Those will all be available until the end of 2024. We also have other new features of Generative AI Hub, for example, the orchestration capability, including prompt templates and data masking. And then we also have some new features with Joule and Kevin is going to tell you more about that.

[Kevin] SAP is going to expand and elevate Joule's capabilities to drive business innovation by providing SAP Joule Studio to create custom skills for Joule, but it also will integrate Joule with SAP HANA Cloud to help you generate code for SAP HANA Cloud and help you work easier with SAP HANA Cloud as well. The SAP Cloud SDK for AI was available for Python already, but now we got that for Java, JavaScript, and ABAP, helping you to integrate the capabilities of SAP Generative AI Hub of SAP AI Core directly into your business enterprise applications.

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 - Summary
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 delivered an inspiring keynote with a strong focus on how technology drives real-world business innovations. This session brought to light the latest advances in AI, cloud solutions, and SAP's vision for the future of enterprise software.

The keynote opened with a thought-provoking introduction about the human brain, its complexities, and how innovation emerges from millions of connections forming new ideas. This metaphor seamlessly tied into SAP's philosophy of turning ideas into tangible solutions—where concepts become tools for solving real business problems.

Innovation at the Heart of SAP’s Strategy

One of the central themes was the growing importance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP’s offerings. AI is now deeply integrated into SAP’s cloud-driven portfolio, designed to simplify customers' lives and meet evolving business needs. Senior AI Scientists at SAP are working on harnessing AI to make business software smarter and more intuitive. The keynote also emphasized SAP's efforts to ensure that its software is configurable, extensible, and unified, providing a seamless suite-like experience across its platforms.

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

A significant highlight was SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP), which has seen rapid growth in customer adoption. BTP is the cornerstone for SAP’s cloud and AI-driven future, empowering businesses with application development, automation, data analytics, and AI capabilities all within one unified platform. The keynote highlighted that over 27,000 live customers and 2,800 partners are leveraging SAP BTP to drive innovation.

The announcement of new BTP regions running on infrastructure from partners like AWS, Microsoft, and Google, along with SAP-managed data centers, shows SAP’s commitment to providing broader regional coverage and reliable service. This expansion aims to help businesses operate globally while maintaining efficiency, security, and compliance.

Enhancements in Application Development and Automation

SAP unveiled its Clean Core Strategy, which allows businesses to build custom extensions in a way that keeps the core ERP system maintainable and upgradeable. With SAP’s low-code environment, developers can create AI-powered applications with ease, while ABAP Cloud now supports both on-stack and side-by-side development, giving businesses more flexibility in their customizations.

One exciting feature introduced was the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, a tool designed to streamline the process of creating custom fields and extensions, allowing users to enhance SAP applications with minimal coding.

SAP Build and GenAI

In keeping with the AI theme, SAP showcased the GenAI capabilities within SAP Build. With Joule, an AI assistant, developers can create complex data models, build extensions, and generate applications through natural language prompts. This AI-driven approach reduces manual coding and helps users integrate advanced functionalities like time series forecasting and predictive analytics into their business processes.

Data and Analytics Innovations

The keynote further highlighted innovations in data and analytics, specifically focusing on SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Datasphere. The HANA Cloud Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph Engine were introduced, both of which enable businesses to harness structured and unstructured data for more context-aware AI-driven decisions. These engines promise faster and more accurate data processing, particularly in scenarios involving large language models and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

Additionally, the keynote showcased how SAP Datasphere is being adopted by large enterprises like Hershey’s and Bosch for data cataloging, integration, and self-service analytics, empowering businesses to access trusted data with ease.

Integration Suite and AI-Driven Optimization

The SAP Integration Suite now offers a modern, AI-powered solution for real-time end-to-end business process integration. With thousands of pre-built integrations and connectors, businesses can seamlessly integrate SAP and third-party applications. SAP is also leveraging AI-generated recommendations to optimize custom scripts, further enhancing developer productivity and reducing memory consumption.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 demonstrated SAP’s unwavering commitment to making innovation accessible, impactful, and transformative. From the integration of advanced AI capabilities to the expansion of BTP, SAP is setting a new standard in enterprise software. Whether you are looking to enhance your application development, automate processes, or harness the power of data, SAP’s latest innovations offer powerful tools to bring your ideas to life.

As SAP continues to evolve, the future of enterprise technology is more connected, intelligent, and adaptable than ever before.

Here is the Keynote from SAP:

Comment below your thoughts on TechEd.

entry.title: SAP Developer News: TechEd Day 2
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

SAP TechEd General

  • News Guide: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide.html
  • TechEd Watch Live: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

Data and Analytics

  • Data and Analytics News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/data-and-analytics.html
  • SAP HANA Cloud Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.offeringsproducts=1693018299239028TpZb

Integration

  • Integration News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/integration.html
  • Integration Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.track=1692371026432005VcKa

New SAP Build Capabilities

  • SAP Build Extensibility Wizard: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-extensibility-wizard-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13867427
  • AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1721791179884001rYRe
  • AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1722394882377001d2M9

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 TechEd Intro
1:13 Data and Analytics
2:25 Integration
3:49 New SAP Build Capabilities

Transcript

[Intro] It's a long way from Philly to Heidelberg, but the show must go on. We're late, aren't we? Yeah, we're late. We all know there's nothing like the real thing. Luckily, we've got our best people on the job. Oh, sorry, Josh! Josh, if you pulled your own weight, we wouldn't have to carry you through this place.

[Witalij] There were many extremely interesting announcements related to data and analytics during SAP TechEd Virtual. Some of them are coming in Q1 of 2025, while others are already available. For example, you might have noticed that as technology continues to evolve, we've also brought SAP Analytics Cloud to Apple Vision Pro. We also announced new data lake capabilities for SAP DataSphere. These new capabilities, including Object Store, Spark processing, and SQL on files, will be available starting in Q4 2024. Looking like I'll need to develop new CodeJam exercises and hit the road with them in 2025. I've done a lot of CodeJam focused on getting started with HANA ML capabilities, and I'm already excited that the development of intelligent data apps will become even more efficient with the introduction of the generative AI toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud. I hope you've seen this in the announcements.

Additionally, the new introduced Knowledge Graph engine in SAP HANA Cloud, combined with the accelerated vector engine, will be a powerful combo for building even more impactful AI-based applications using SAP data. So lots of work and fun ahead. Stay with us.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. Many announcements in the integration space as part of SAP TechEd 2024. So we have new integration adapters to easily connect to ANAPLAN, CUPA, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake, and more. We also have a new message status overview monitor. That's the aggregated overview of the status messages, for example, that we had in SAP Process Orchestration that we all love. The migration tool has been enhanced. Now it's possible to do mass migration of integration scenarios. There's a new licensing option for SAP Integration Suite, which is called Starter Edition. This is focused for grow with SAP customers and SAP SuccessFactors customers. Also, we have new generative AI capabilities in SAP Integration Suite. So we can now optimize our groovy scripts in SAP Cloud Integration. And finally, the product, SAP Event Broker for SAP Cloud Applications, was recently renamed to SAP Cloud Application Event Hub. Apart from that, you can check out the documentation in CAP to see how you can easily connect your CAP application deployed to SAP BTP to the SAP Application Event Hub.

[Shrini] SAP announces several improvements to SAP Build. Its comprehensive solution for extending S/4 HANA Cloud and other line of business applications. The most significant updates enhance SAP Build by streamlining S/4 HANA extension developments, introducing new generative AI capabilities, and expanding access to about development tools. A new extension wizard available now lets developers access SAP Build directly from S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition. With this access, developers can extend custom fields, business logic, and processes, either on-stack or side-by-side. A critical feature of this integration is the knowledge of business context, such as events and objects, when creating business processes and while transitioning between S/4 HANA and SAP brand. This preserves important business information, enabling developers to switch between environments seamlessly without losing crucial context.

In Q4 2024, the extension wizard will let developers create SAP Fiori and SAPUI5 applications, leveraging the full set of features available in SAP Build. In Q4 2024, new capabilities such as code exploration and documentation search in SAP Build Code will reduce development time for Java and JavaScript developers. Low-code developers and business users will also be able to create user interfaces and automate workflows with assistance from Juul. Joule is SAP's generative AI assistant. In Q1 2025, ABAP developers will be able to generate high-quality code and unit tests with Joule that comply with ABAP cloud development model. Joule will also be able to generate explanations for legacy code, making it easier to modernize legacy code bases and migrate to a clean code. By end of Q4 2024, Joule will also be integrated into SAP Build Work Zone, a low-code tool for creating engaging and personalized business sites. Joule's generative AI capabilities will offer user task automation and intelligent support.

[Sheena] By end of 2024, developers will also be able to use low-code capabilities in SAP build to enhance Joule with new skills, integrating it seamlessly with both SAP and non-SAP applications. This flexibility ensures Joule can be tailored to meet customer-specific business needs. Finally, developers can create applications that feature alternative AI capabilities by taking full advantage of HANA Cloud to ground large language models using its vector engine. Soon, developers will also be able to achieve this S/4 HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine, scheduled for general availability in the first half of 2025. By the end of 2024, SAP Build will include access to ABAP development tools and ABAP development environments for SAP S/4 HANA Cloud and SAP BTP. New access from the SAP Build Lobby, the solution's central starting point for all extension projects, will make it easier for multi-disciplinary development teams, also known as Fusion Teams, to build extensions and custom processes across all technology stacks. Developers will also be able to create and monitor their web cloud projects directly from SAP Build. This integration streamlines workflows by reducing the need to switch between different environments, significantly improving development efficiency.

entry.title: SAP Teched ---- I was worried this year... On-Premise - Favorite Session
entry.content:
SAP Teched!   It is one of my favorite events.   But lately...  lately - I've been kind of... well not excited.    My company is On-Premise.  I was worried none of it would work with my current job.   Then I gave myself a pep talk.   I remembered that I went to Teched to see all the possibilities.  And decide if there was anything I needed to learn immediately.

Well...  I was excited about all the new things.   I always am.   Then I heard something that really made going to the event worth it for me.   SAP does understand that we have many applications that were custom built.    There was something cool called Tier 3.  I need to read more on it.   I am not the expert by any stretch of the imagination.   Here's what I heard - Our old developments are safe.   We don't have to just drop them all and try to rewrite them quickly in a better way.   The idea is to gradually move to the "new" way of doing things, and phase out our old programs.  We'll actually have the time to move to the "clean core" way of doing things.   The session I was at when I heard that was "Clean Core extensibility SAP S/4 private cloud".   

Big sigh - and happy face from me.  I have developed so much code.   Code is being used now.  It doesn't need to change immediately.  It can be done over time.   Plus as a bonus we get a friend that will help us - like they are sitting right next to you to help you understand how to do something. The AI capability built for all of us - Joule.  Also help you find where that pesky data is hiding.

It was interesting to me that many people were wondering if JAVA and ABAP developers would be obsolete in the future.  My answer to myself was no of course not!  We still have a lot of old code.   Then I look at Joule and think about how many interesting things we can write with the basics taken care of by the AI software.   So maybe in time - the old languages will go away.   If they do, that's OK too.   I new other languages before.   No big change for me.   What has changed is AI.  Joule can spring board us into using all the fun new tools!  My learning time could be significantly reduced.  Then when thought about - I thought of all the repetitive tasks we do.  If they were eliminated, I would have time to work on the "harder" solutions.

Anyway - my short quick recap.   There was a ton to learn.  (as usual)   Another fun fact - on the exercises.  I can do them later on  my own time.  (YES! Github)  That's a big plus.  Honestly, it's going to be a while before I do that.   But once I do - I'll be happy to share my experience.

What did you learn about?

entry.title: Come Say Hi at an SAP TechEd Tour Stop
entry.content:
It’s a wrap! Yes, sadly, SAP TechEd Virtual is already over, but it’s been a blast! But the good news is: If you want to continue to learn, network, and bring out your best, you can still participate in one of five SAP TechEd on Tour or SAPinsider events worldwide – with learning sessions and meet-ups with SAP experts. Here’s what you need to know to come and say “Hi”: 

SAP is offering five separate tour stops around the globe, organized in collaboration with SAP user groups. Each stop will offer hands-on learning opportunities, deep-dive lecture sessions, and meet-the-experts all presented by SAP experts, partners, and many customers. We are also bringing back the Developer Garage for you as a great opportunity to learn by doing. 

Choose the US or Europe to meet SAP experts 

From November 12-14, join technical practitioners and SAP experts at ASUG Tech Connect 2024 in West Palm Beach for immersive sessions, SAP insights, and networking. Over three days, you'll enhance your skills, stay at the forefront of digital advancements, and grow professionally. 

If you prefer a trip to Europe, SAPinsider EMEA 2024 in Copenhagen, Denmark, is for you. From November 12-14, you can develop the skills and expertise needed to thrive in your role. Hear from customers about their successful digital transformations, learn implementation best practices from SAP partners, and connect with peers and SAP experts in a three-day learning event aligned with SAP TechEd Virtual.  

Get an overview of the SAP ecosystem in Australia 

If you fancy a trip to Down Under, we’re excited to meet you at SAUG-x 2024 this year! From November 19-20 you can learn from unique insights, network with your peers, hear from SAP experts, and get an overview of our ecosystem. Don’t miss these relevant knowledge sharing, learning, and networking sessions – all in Melbourne, Australia! 

And if you’re still in the mood for SAP TechEd by December, join us in Birmingham, UK, at UKISUG Connect 2024. At the ICC in Birmingham, expect to learn from experts, network with like-minded peers and collaborate together. Benefit from knowledge-rich keynotes, engaging breakout sessions, a range of workshops and an exhibition hall of over 70 SAP partners. 

Last, but not least, head over to the motherland of SAP – Germany – and join us at DSAG-TechXchange 2025 on April 4, 2025! Yes, it might be a couple of months away, but having your next trip planned is always a nice thing, don’t you think? 

Whichever location you prefer – we are looking forward to seeing you there! Make use of the unique opportunity to connect with like-minded individuals, gain valuable insights into the tech industry, and get hands-on experience with the latest SAP technologies. Whether you're a developer, architect, IT manager, or business executive, SAP TechEd on Tour has something for everyone. Don't miss out on this opportunity to stay ahead of the curve and propel your tech career to new heights! 

For more information on the SAP TechEd on Tour stops, visit this page. 

And while you’re at it, make sure to attend SAP CodeJam events in your area - free, hands-on coding experiences where you can collaborate with fellow developers to solve real-world challenges while diving deep into the latest SAP technologies.

entry.title: Top 10 takeaways #SAPTeched 2024
entry.content:
1. Harnessing GenAI: The True Power Lies in Its Application

"You know how they say we can only access 20% of our brain? This lets you access all of it!" That's what Vernon Gant told Eddie Morra when he gave him the NZT-48 pill in the movie Limitless. Since OpenAI released ChatGPT in November 2022, large language models (LLMs) and other foundational models have gone mainstream—giving everyone their own version of NZT-48.

The question is: What do people bring to the table? Are they like Eddie, with a solid foundation in math and law that needs a boost? Or like Gennady, whose extortion skills got turbocharged? This analogy applies to companies as well.

SAP has a solid head start with its Business Technology Platform (BTP). At TechEd, SAP announced a slew of AI-enabled advancements that would have been impossible just 23 months ago.

Screen Capture of the Movie 'limitless' Released 2011

2. Embracing Innovation Through SAP BTP

As technology evolves, building new code or introducing innovative approaches depends on a strong platform foundation. SAP has fully embraced this with its Business Technology Platform (BTP). Originally launched as SAP Cloud Platform, BTP has evolved into a unified platform that consolidates essential components to help businesses scale, innovate, and thrive.

BTP's key pillars—application development, automation, integration, data and analytics, and AI—are driving innovation across industries, enabling companies to adapt to changing needs. With 27,000 live customers and over 2,800 partners, SAP BTP's success continues to grow, empowering businesses to stay competitive.

SAP BTP Portfolio 2024

3. AI Foundation on BTP: Relevant, Reliable, Responsible

"Relevant, Reliable, Responsible" is not just a buzzword for SAP.  SAP has taken significant actions to demonstrate this commitment.

  • Responsible: The AI Foundation model is expanding access to more LLMs. A highlight this year is Mistral Large 2, which will be hosted in SAP's own data centers rather than relying on external APIs, ensuring complete control over your data. Additionally, SAP has announced access to the latest LLMs from Aleph Alpha, Meta, and IBM.
  • Reliable: The SAP Gen AI Hub now offers various orchestration tools—such as data masking, grounding, and content filtering—to ensure that outputs from LLMs are trustworthy and less prone to hallucinations.
  • Relevant: SAP has built a comprehensive set of AI services, AI lifecycle management, and business data and content for AI, allowing seamless integration into SAP applications—all accessible via Joule.

SAP AI Foundation

4. Joule Updates: Your Trusted Co-Pilot

Joule, SAP's intelligent co-pilot, turns one this year! It will cover 80% of commonly used workflows and tasks in SAP, featuring 500 skills today with a goal to reach 1,200 by year-end, spanning HR, finance, sales, supply chain, and more.

New announcements include:

  • Multiple Language Support: Joule now supports multiple languages, expanding its accessibility.
  • Expanded Data Centers: Increased infrastructure to support Joule's growing capabilities.
  • Availability in SAP Mobile Start: Joule is now accessible in SAP Mobile Start.
  • Joule Studio in SAP Build: Enabling two million ABAP developers globally, Joule now supports ABAP developer capabilities in Eclipse.

Future Outlook:

Joule Agents: Specialized in specific domains like HR or Customer service, these agents leverage multi-agent systems, allowing businesses to create custom skills, including integration with other enterprise apps like ServiceNow via APIs. This expansion ensures Joule remains a powerful co-pilot, enabling seamless transactional, informational, and analytical interactions to boost business efficiency and automation.

Joule Agents

5. Unified Application Development Under SAP Build, Including ABAP

Two years ago, SAP introduced a low-code environment and last year added AI-powered code development for Java and JavaScript. This year, the latest SAP Build release includes ABAP Cloud, which comes with its own large language model trained on 25 million lines of ABAP code, offering immense power for the SAP ecosystem.

What does this mean? You can now extend your core S/4HANA systems using an AI-powered approach in ABAP Cloud in two ways:

  • On-Stack: Build custom extensions directly within the core system.
  • Side-by-Side: Develop modular, customizable extensions on SAP BTP.

The key is to maintain a clean core. The goal is to make all applications lifecycle-compliant, meaning they are built to last without requiring adaptations during innovation cycles. This applies to both private and public cloud. For private cloud, SAP Build supports RFC connectivity, enabling seamless third-party integration with S/4HANA. For public cloud, there is the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, which helps key users and developers create both on-stack and side-by-side extensions within S/4HANA in an embedded experience.

ABAP Cloud in SAP Build

6. Data in the GenAI Age

In the era of Generative AI, creating intelligent applications requires careful data handling. While foundational models perform well, enterprise data needs precise management.

  • HANA Cloud Vector Engine: enables in-database vectorization with a proprietary SAP-specific text model, simplifying the creation of vector embeddings. This allows LLMs to provide more accurate, context-aware outputs for enterprise use. A notable example is Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), where the engine retrieves relevant business data to improve AI results.
  • Knowledge Graph Engine: This engine organizes data as interconnected facts, helping users navigate relationships and extract insights easily. In the GenAI space, knowledge graphs can power domain-specific question-answering systems, combining business data from manuals, documents, and records to generate precise, context-aware responses.
  • GenAI Toolkit for HANA Cloud: Similar to ABAP Cloud's AI-enabled code generation, this toolkit provides users access via Joule, allowing them to query datasets and receive results through AI-driven interactions.

SAP Knowledge Graph

7. Analytics in the GenAI age

Both SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud have received significant enhancements this year.

  • SAP Analytics Cloud now features SAP Analytics Cloud Compass, set to be generally available in Q1 2025. This feature enables business users to model complex risk scenarios using the Monte Carlo simulation method. With a user-friendly interface, non-technical users can perform real-time risk analysis without needing prior setup or technical expertise.
  • SAP Datasphere gets a boost with new embedded data lake capabilities, including the Integrated Object Store for storing large amounts of data, Spark Data Processing capabilities, and SQL Files for live access to data in the integrated object store without duplication. These features allow organizations of all sizes to implement a business data fabric in a simple, cost-efficient, and scalable way, powered by SAP HANA Cloud.

SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datashpere

8. SAP Integration Suite Enhancements

Many SAP customers leveraging SAP Integration Suite have experienced a significant return on investment—up to 345%, according to Forrester.

In the era of Generative AI, you can now optimize your existing integrations with AI-generated recommendations. This allows you to run script optimizations, modify connections, relaunch applications, and immediately observe the changes in system performance after the optimizations, providing a significant boost. (This feature will be available as part of SAP Integration Suite Premium Edition.)

Additionally, there's a new commercial offering: the SAP Integration Suite Starter Edition for SAP SuccessFactors and SAP GROW customers. This edition provides access to all pre-packaged SAP integrations, along with a fixed number of customizable integrations.

SAP Integration Suite

9. Business Transformation Management

LeanIX, Signavio, and WalkMe now form a cohesive block where customers need it most—business transformation management. With LeanIX, you can automatically discover your current application landscape. Signavio helps you discover and design your business processes, and WalkMe enables people to seamlessly navigate and adopt these processes, supporting activities such as employee onboarding, performance management, and job changes in SAP SuccessFactors.

SAP Business Transformation Management

10. AI Use Cases in Action Across Applications

A sample customer, "TeachIT Technologies," was used to demonstrate the power of Generative AI in various application spaces. The scenario: TeachIT Technologies, a mid-sized software implementation company, is facing challenges in project management, resource allocation, and international expansion.

  • Cloud ERP: Project managers track success criteria with GenAI-powered in-app extensibility. SAP BTP allows for custom application development, streamlining processes without manual coding.
  • UI and AI-Assisted Development: Developers use SAP Fiori Tools and LLMs to quickly transform sketches into functional applications, simplifying the development process (future-looking).
  • Global Expansion & Compliance: SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) and GenAI automatically configure regional business processes for expansion, ensuring compliance with local regulations. AI in SAP’s compliance tools resolves tax errors like incorrect GST numbers.
  • Sales & Operations: SAP Sales Cloud and SuccessFactors use AI tools to help project managers manage teams, set goals, and track budgets, making decision-making easier.

Almost all applications, in one form or another, have their own 'NZT-48 pill'!

Please explore these in the newly launched SAP Business AI Features in SAP Discovery center.

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/index.html

SAP Business AI Features

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 Announcements - Consolidated and Simplified
entry.content:
In this blog, I have consolidated everything related to SAP TechEd 2024 in one place! Hope this will help the community.

Note: Don't miss the list of all the links of news, recordings, source-code, demo etc. towards the end of this blog.

 

First let's have a quick summary of all the key announcements.

 

 

Let's go in detail.

SAP Build + ABAP Cloud

  • SAP Build unifies the entire development and extension capabilities and will now also include ABAP Cloud.
  • Technically, it means that SAP Build will integrate ABAP development tools and ABAP development environments for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP BTP.
  • Developers will also be able to create and monitor their ABAP Cloud projects directly from SAP Build.
  • It will be available by Q4 2024.
  • SAP Build Lobby will be the central starting point for all extension projects.

Demo of SAP Build Lobby

 

SAP Build for S/4HANA Extension Development

  • A new extensibility wizard is available now which will let developers access SAP Build directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
  • With this, developers can extend custom fields, business logic and processes either on-stack (deployed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ABAP environment) or side-by-side (deployed in SAP BTP).
  • In Q4 2024, the extensibility wizard will let developers create custom SAP Fiori and SAPUI5 applications, leveraging the full set of features available in SAP Build.

 

New Generative AI Capabilities of SAP Build with Joule

  • New capabilities in SAP Build Code such as code explanation and documentation search will reduce development time for Java and JavaScript developers (Q4 2024).
  • Developers will also be able to automate workflows in SAP Build Process Automation with assistance from generative AI (Q4 2024)
  • ABAP developers will be able to generate high-quality code and unit tests with Joule that comply with SAP’s ABAP Cloud Development model, and also generate explanations for legacy code, making it easier to modernize legacy codebases and migrate to a clean core (Q1 2025)
  • Developers will also be able to enhance Joule with custom skills using Joule studio in SAP Build, integrating it seamlessly with both SAP and non-SAP applications (Q1 2025)

For more information on SAP Build, refer to SAP Build.

 

Joule with SAP Build Work Zone

  • Joule will also be integrated into SAP Build Work Zone (Q4 2024)
  • It will be available in SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, the SAP Start site and the SAP Mobile Start app.

 

Joule with Collaborative AI Agents

  • SAP will infuse Joule with multiple autonomous AI agents that will combine their unique expertise to collaboratively accomplish complex workflows (Q4 2024)
  • With an in-built agentic capability, Joule can now solve complex business problems for you through planning, self-reflection, logical reasoning and collaboration.
  • Developers will be able to extend Joule by building their own autonomous AI agent use cases with SAP Build (Q1 2025)

Joule Agent Demo

 

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Joule will be available for all SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers (Q4 2024)
  • Joule capabilities will expand to support key functions including sales professionals, warehouse clerks and project managers (Q1 of 2025)

 

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

  • Following the introduction of Joule into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in July, SAP launched a feature pack with new use cases that let business users instruct Joule with simple commands.
  • The feature pack includes a new implementation wizard that installs a model outlining criteria to evaluate extensions built with low-code, no-code and pro-code tools on SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP.
  • Joule will be incorporated into the top 300 most-frequently-used SAP Fiori apps (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will also be able to take master data change requests within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, so users don’t have to navigate across multiple menus.

 

Joule in SAP BTP

  • Joule will be available to use with SAP HANA Cloud in SAP BTP cockpit (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be incorporated into SAP BTP cockpit (Q4 2024)
  • Joule can be directed to perform transactional scenarios like creating a subaccount, onboarding users, and answering questions about SAP BTP administrations in general. 

 

Joule in Other SAP Solutions

  • Joule will be available in the SAP Service Cloud solution and SAP Concur solutions (Q1 2025)
  • SAP now offers Joule across the SAP Integrated Business Planning solution, the SAP Digital Manufacturing solution, and the SAP Asset Performance Management application.
  • SAP will continue to roll out Joule across the entire SAP Supply Chain portfolio.
  • Joule will be available in the SAP Sales Cloud solution with navigational and task-management assistance (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will help SAP LeanIX solutions users manage and optimize their IT landscape (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be available with SAP Signavio (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be available in SAP Mobile Start (Q4 2024)      

 

Joule studio in SAP Build

  • SAP will release Joule studio in SAP Build (Q4 2024).
  • Joule studio will provide a dedicated environment for businesses to create, deploy, monitor and manage custom skills for Joule developed with low-code, no-code capabilities.
  • Custom skills, which complement Joule’s out-of-the-box capabilities from SAP, extend conversational AI to organization-specific workflows.
  • Additionally, customers can now integrate any third-party system into Joule for an entirely integrated conversational user experience.

 

SAP Knowledge Graph

  • SAP announces the addition of a knowledge graph engine to SAP HANA Cloud (Q1 2025)
  • The knowledge graph engine will let users identify and understand complex relationships in their data not otherwise discoverable using traditional data modeling tools.
  • SAP announces SAP Knowledge Graph, a new solution that will unlock the full value of SAP data by connecting it with the rich business context captured in SAP applications.
  • Accessible through SAP Datasphere and Joule in Q1 2025, it will help root AI in SAP-specific business semantics, reducing the risk of inaccurate or irrelevant results.
  • In its initial release, this solution will focus on SAP S/4HANA, providing a semantic layer that connects over 450,000 ABAP tables, 80,000 CDS views and thousands of SAP Fiori applications, along with OData APIs and entity sets.

 

SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine

  • Knowledge graph engine will be added to SAP HANA Cloud (Q1 2025)
  • By combining the vector engine and the knowledge graph engine, you can harness the power of structured and unstructured data together.

 

SAP Expands Strategic AI Partnerships

  • Anthropic: SAP introduces Anthropic’s Claude 3.5 Sonnet in Amazon Bedrock to generative AI hub.
  • HCLTech: HCLTech announces a newly built AI Center of Excellence in Munich focused on developing AI use cases for the AI Foundation on SAP BTP.
  • IBM: SAP announces the addition of IBM Granite, IBM’s flagship series of transparent and efficient LLMs, to the growing repository of AI models available in generative AI hub.
  • Meta: SAP continues to execute its multi-vendor strategy for LLMs with the addition of Meta Llama 3.1 70B, which is built to deliver performance and cost efficiency.
  • Mistral AI: SAP continues to expand its partnership with Mistral AI by adding Mistral Large 2 to generative AI hub.
  • PwC: PwC announces it is using generative AI capabilities based on SAP’s generative AI hub.

 

Generative AI Hub + ABAP

  • Developers will be able to access generative AI hub capabilities from custom ABAP applications using the ABAP AI SDK powered by the intelligent scenario lifecycle management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud functionality. 
  • Using the ABAP AI SDK, ABAP developers can easily infuse AI capabilities into their custom applications and extensions.

 

Generative AI Hub – New LLMs Support

New LLMs will be available for generative AI hub by the end of 2024, including:

  • Aleph Alpha Pharia-1
  • Amazon Titan Image Generator
  • IBM Granite
  • Mistral Large 2
  • OpenAI Dall-E 3

 

Generative AI Hub – New Data Centers

New data centers for generative AI hub by the end of Q4 2024:

  • AWS: Singapore
  • Google Cloud Platform: Australia (Sydney); Japan (Osaka)
  • Microsoft Azure: Australia (Sydney); Switzerland (Zurich)

 

Generative AI Hub – Other Updates

  • We will be able to integrate advanced AI capabilities into web apps with new SDK support for ABAP, Java, and JavaScript in generative AI hub (Q4 2024)
  • Orchestration improvements to the generative AI hub capability in SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad that will enable document grounding and indexing pipelines (Q4 2024)

 

New generative AI functionality for customers move to the cloud faster

  • SAP announced a new generative AI functionality that helps customers move to the cloud faster (Q1 2025).
  • Accessed through the SAP Cloud ALM solution, customers and consultants will be able to get expert answers, proactive guidance, and task automation aligned with the RISE with SAP Methodology.

 

SAP Expands its Portfolio of Learning and Certification Offerings

  • SAP launches new role-based certifications, free training materials and hands-on opportunities for developers and tech professionals.
  • These includes 60 new self-paced learning journeys, trial experiences and solution demonstrations that help tech professionals benefit from the latest fully integrated and practical training.

 

SAP Datasphere

  • SAP announced new embedded data lake capabilities for SAP Datasphere (Q4 2024)
  • Built on SAP HANA Cloud SQL on files and data lake file service capabilities, it will expand the business data fabric architecture with a data lake option, complementing existing storage solutions.
  • This helps businesses access, manage, and analyze data across cloud and hybrid environments while preserving its context and logic, improving decision-making, operational efficiency, and AI-driven innovation.

 

New embedded data lake capabilities

  • An integrated object store, which provides a simpler way to store large amounts of data, making it easier for businesses to expand their storage as needed.
  • Spark compute, which facilitates efficient data transformation and processing based on existing SAP Datasphere data integration capabilities.
  • SQL on files functionality, which lets developers access data in the integrated object store without needing to physically copy the data.

These capabilities let users import and integrate data at scale while maintaining a consistent user experience that ensures seamless data integration from both SAP sources (including SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Warehouse) and non-SAP sources while preserving the original structure and context of the data.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud

  • SAP announced general availability of the new SAP Analytics Cloud compass feature (Q1 2025)
  • SAP Analytics Cloud compass feature can be used to model complex risk scenarios and simulate a broad range of probable outcomes.

 

SAP Integration Suite

  • SAP Integration Suite, starter edition will offer an unlimited number of prepackaged SAP integrations and a fixed number of customizable integrations for GROW with SAP and SAP SuccessFactors customers (Q4 2024)
  • New integration adapters will allow users manage data from various third-party technology platforms, including Anaplan, Coupa, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake, and more.
  • AI supported custom script optimization (2025)
  • AI support iFlow creation is now Generally Available

 

SAP TechEd 2024 - Recordings and News
